name: Agree.com
founder: Marty Ringlein, Will Hubbard, Evan Dudla
tagline: AI-powered e-signature platform with built-in invoicing and payment processing that makes e-signatures free.
why: Founded Feb 2024 by serial founders who previously sold companies to Twitter, Eventbrite and Brex. Grew from 0 to 25,000+ users (Beehiiv, Product Hunt, Rho) within months of launch by giving e-signatures away free and monetizing payments. $7.2M seed led by Pelion was oversubscribed and closed in two weeks.

name: Fanbase
tagline: Sports-specific platform managing fan data, mobile apps, subscriptions and ticketing for teams, leagues and venues.
why: Part of the wave of sector-specific software replacing generic tools in sport. Customers span soccer clubs, national governing bodies like British Handball and leagues including the Scottish Women's Premier League. Named to SportsPro's 2025 list of sports tech ideas to invest in now.

name: Zatap
tagline: 'Phygital' platform using NFC tags and QR codes to connect physical products to digital content, rewards and authenticity.
why: Rebranded from collectID in 2024 and raised a $3.5M seed led by SeventySix Capital and Hellen's Rock. Already counts the NHL's Nashville Predators, AS Monaco and the McLaren, Alpine and Williams F1 teams among clients, unlocking direct-to-consumer relationships for brands.

name: Magic AI
tagline: An intelligent fitness mirror delivering personalised workouts and real-time feedback without a personal trainer.
why: Raised a $5M Series A led by IW Capital with executives from TikTok, Meta and Spotify investing. Users have logged 46,000+ hours of training and its workout library features athletes such as former England cricket captain Sir Alastair Cook.

name: Lucra Sports
tagline: White-label gamification platform that lets brands add friendly, skill-based wagering to their apps and loyalty programs.
why: Applies the gamification of betting to no-stakes activities, letting businesses embed competition into consumer apps. Raised $10M in 2024 from SeventySix Capital and NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo to expand its platform and enter the UK.

name: Onos Health
tagline: AI tool that helps health plans analyse payer data and make better behavioral health clinical decisions.
why: Tackles one of the messiest areas of US healthcare-how insurers manage behavioral health. Raised a $6.3M seed in October 2025 co-led by Haystack and Pathlight Ventures to scale its payer-facing analytics.

name: Fastbreak AI
tagline: AI-powered sports operations engine that automates scheduling and competition management for leagues and tournaments.
why: Uses mathematical optimisation to solve the notoriously hard problem of season scheduling. Backers and customers include the NBA, NHL and TMRW Sports, and it raised a $40M Series A in late 2025 (Greycroft, GTMfund) to expand across pro and amateur sports.

name: Tala Health
tagline: Vertically integrated virtual care platform pairing AI agents with licensed clinicians across the patient journey.
why: One of the largest healthtech seed rounds ever-$100M at a $1.2B valuation led by Sofreh Capital with Dr. P. Roy Vagelos participating. Founded by Ritankar Das to combine AI agents and clinicians for symptom evaluation, referral and follow-up care at scale.

name: Lovable
tagline: AI 'vibe coding' platform that lets anyone build functional software and apps by chatting in natural language.
why: One of Europe's fastest-growing startups: founded late 2023, it raised a $200M Series A at a $1.8B valuation in 2025 after rapid ARR growth. Democratises software creation by removing the need to write code.

name: H Company
founder: Laurent Sifre, Charles Kantor, Daan Wierstra, Karl Tuyls, Julien Perolat
tagline: Builds 'action-oriented' AI agents that automate enterprise workflows and productivity tasks.
why: Closed a record $220M seed in 2024-at the time the largest AI raise in Europe-founded by a team of ex-DeepMind and Google researchers. Positioned to compete in the fast-growing enterprise AI agent market.

name: Resolve AI
tagline: An autonomous AI site reliability engineer that detects, diagnoses, and fixes production software incidents in real time.
why: Founded by ex-Splunk observability execs, Resolve hit a $1B valuation in its Series A about 18 months after founding. The $125M round was led by Lightspeed with participation from Greylock (which led the prior $35M seed alongside Fei-Fei Li and Jeff Dean) and Unusual Ventures.

name: 7AI
tagline: A platform of 40+ autonomous AI agents that conduct enterprise security investigations at scale.
why: Founded by the Cybereason co-founders, 7AI raised what it calls the largest cybersecurity Series A in history ($130M) about 10 months after exiting stealth. The round was led by Index Ventures with participation from Greylock, CRV, Spark Capital, and Blackstone Innovations Investments.

name: Artisan
tagline: An all-in-one outbound sales platform powered by 'AI employees' like Ava, an autonomous BDR.
why: Artisan became widely known via its viral 'Stop Hiring Humans' campaign and raised a $25M Series A in April 2025 led by Glade Brook Capital, with Y Combinator, HubSpot Ventures, and Day One Ventures participating after an earlier $11.5M seed that included Sequoia. Total funding is about $46M.

name: Fable Security
tagline: An AI-native human risk management platform that delivers real-time, behavior-based security interventions to employees.
why: Founded by two early Abnormal AI team members, Fable launched out of stealth in July 2025 with $31M total across a Greylock-led $6.5M seed and a Redpoint Ventures-led $24.5M Series A. The female-led team targets the human layer of enterprise cybersecurity, backed by two top-tier funds.

name: Sandstone
founder: Nick Fleisher, Jarryd Strydom, Liam Germain
tagline: An AI-native platform for in-house legal teams that pulls in data from HR, sales, and other departments to keep counsel informed.
why: Sandstone launched with a $10M seed led by Sequoia Capital. CEO Nick Fleisher previously led the legal technology practice at McKinsey, and the company is positioning itself as the operating layer for AI-native legal departments. Sequoia's backing at public launch is a strong early signal.

name: Adaptive ML
founder: Julien Launay, Baptiste Pannier, Daniel Hesslow
tagline: An enterprise RLOps platform that lets companies customize and operate open-source LLMs with reinforcement learning.
why: Founded by the team behind the Falcon and BLOOM open-source models, formerly of Hugging Face, Adaptive ML raised a $20M seed led by Index Ventures with ICONIQ Capital and Databricks Ventures at roughly a $100M valuation. The founders' foundation-model pedigree is a standout signal.

name: LegalFly
founder: Ruben Miessen, Kasper Verbeeck, Dennis Montegnies, Gregory Vekemans
tagline: A secure legal AI workspace with agents that automate contract analysis, regulatory compliance, and document management.
why: Built by former product leaders from Tinder, LegalFly raised a EUR 15M+ Series A led by Notion Capital with participation from redalpine and Fortino Capital. The team's consumer-scale product background plus a security-first enterprise legal AI product gives it a differentiated angle.

name: Pit
founder: Adam Jafer, Filip Lindvall, Fredrik Hjelm, Anton Oberg, Fredrik Olovsson
tagline: An 'AI product team as a service' that learns how an enterprise operates and builds custom production software for its internal workflows.
why: Co-founded by the team behind European scooter giant Voi (plus ex-Klarna and iZettle engineers), Pit exited stealth in 2026 with a $16M seed led by a16z, with Lakestar and angels from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Revolut. Early customers include Voi, Kry, and Stena Recycling.

name: HealthEx
tagline: A consumer consent and preference platform that unlocks compliant access to health data for patients and health systems.
why: Led by General Catalyst with participation from Electric Capital. Founders are repeat operators: Agarwal (ex-UCSF, MyoKardia/BMS) and Raghavan (founding team and VP Eng at BlueJeans, acquired by Verizon), tackling the high-value health data interoperability and consent problem.

name: Radical Health
tagline: Building a general-purpose AI oncologist that personalizes cancer care for patients and clinicians.
why: Pre-seed led by Khosla Ventures with Protagonist, incubated through Entrepreneurs First. The company has exclusive access to large hospital datasets covering about 11.8 million patient journeys, positioning it strongly in precision-oncology AI.

name: TORTUS AI
tagline: An AI clinical co-worker (O.S.L.E.R.) that automates documentation and admin so doctors can spend more time with patients.
why: Seed round led by Khosla Ventures with Entrepreneur First and notable angels including former NHS Chair Lord David Prior. Founded by cardiologist Dr. Dom Pimenta and ML engineer Christopher Tan, TORTUS is rolling out across the UK's NHS.

name: Zingage
tagline: An AI care-delivery platform that automates scheduling, intake, documentation, and billing for home care agencies.
why: Seed round led by Bessemer Venture Partners with TQ Ventures, South Park Commons, and WndrCo. Founders are proven operators (Hunt sold a prior marketplace; Tian was at Ramp and TikTok), and the platform already coordinates about 50,000 caregivers across 400+ agencies.

name: Parallel
tagline: AI agents that operate directly on top of legacy hospital software to automate medical coding, billing, and admissions.
why: Series A led by Index Ventures, with prior backing from Y Combinator, Frst, and Hexa, plus angels including Mistral's Arthur Mensch. CEO Lafforgue is ex-Meta/McKinsey and CTO Rydahl founded Hublo (Europe's largest healthcare staffing platform); the company raised its Series A less than a year after seed.

name: Foundation Health
tagline: AI-powered infrastructure spanning pharmacy operations, care coordination, and direct-to-patient delivery.
why: Series A led by Define Ventures with Intermountain Ventures, Vanderbilt University, and BoxGroup. Founded by Umar Afridi, co-founder of pharmacy-fulfillment company Truepill, giving the team deep domain credibility in the large, under-digitized pharmacy operations market.

name: Daydream
founder: Julie Bornstein, Matt Fisher, Dan Cary, Lisa Yamner Green, Richard Kim
tagline: An AI-powered conversational search and discovery platform that reinvents how people shop for fashion online.
why: Led by Julie Bornstein, an e-commerce veteran (ex-COO of Stitch Fix, ex-Sephora and Nordstrom). It raised one of the largest consumer seed rounds of 2024 at $50M, co-led by Forerunner Ventures and Index Ventures, with GV and True Ventures participating.

name: Infinite Machine
tagline: Designing and building next-generation personal electric vehicles, starting with the cyberpunk-styled P1 urban scooter.
why: Reinventing personal mobility with bold industrial design and a passionate early following for its P1 scooter and Olto e-bike. Its $9.3M seed round was led by Andreessen Horowitz (American Dynamism), with backing from the founders of Reformation, Replit, and Hugging Face, plus F1 champion Nico Rosberg.

name: Sweatpals
tagline: A social fitness platform that helps people build real-life connection and community through in-person workouts.
why: Riding the booming social fitness and run-club trend, building a fast-growing community-driven network. Its $12M seed round was backed by Patron, a16z speedrun, and Kevin Hart's HartBeat Ventures, with participation from WndrCo, Pear, and Antler.

name: Sitch
tagline: An AI-powered matchmaking app that pairs human-style curation with LLMs to set users up on real dates instead of endless swiping.
why: One of the buzziest entrants in the AI-dating wave, founded by Nandini Mullaji (Stanford GSB, early Bumble India team) and Chad DePue (ex-Snapchat, Microsoft). The seed round was led by M13 with participation from a16z speedrun and a strong angel syndicate, totaling about $9M.

name: Claim
tagline: A social rewards network where Gen Z users discover brands, earn cash back, and share and trade rewards with friends.
why: Strong Gen Z traction by turning brand discovery and cash-back rewards into a social experience. Its $4M seed was led by Sequoia Capital (with Susa Ventures and BoxGroup in the pre-seed), and it followed with a $12M Series A led by VMG Technology.

name: Pogo
founder: Dom Wong, Oskar Melking, Shikhar Mohan
tagline: A consumer app that lets users earn money from their own data, now powering an AI consumer-research platform built on purchase-verified buyers.
why: Pogo has amassed 3M+ opted-in users and reportedly delivers seven-figure revenue per employee. It has raised about $32M to date from a top-tier roster including Harry Stebbings' 20VC, Josh Buckley's Buckley Ventures, Mantis (The Chainsmokers), and the founders of Honey.

name: Growl
tagline: An AI-powered, wall-mounted boxing bag that projects a life-sized virtual coach for interactive at-home fitness.
why: Growl raised a $4.75M seed led by Skip Capital with Kima Ventures, Teampact Ventures, and UFC champion Ciryl Gane, positioning itself as the 'Peloton of boxing.' Its founding team includes talent from Peloton, Tonal, and Aescape, and an oversubscribed pre-order campaign signals early consumer traction.

name: Peripheral Labs
tagline: An AI neural-rendering platform that turns standard broadcast feeds into navigable 3D volumetric replays in near real-time.
why: Peripheral Labs raised a $3.6M seed led by Khosla Ventures with Daybreak Capital, Entrepreneurs First, and Transpose Platform. The founders led the University of Toronto's self-driving racing teams and repurposed autonomous-vehicle sensor tech for sports.

name: Fanstake
founder: Greg Glass, Donnie Flood, Alex Boisvert
tagline: A fan-powered NIL marketplace where college sports fans pledge money to recruit and retain athletes, paying only if the athlete commits.
why: Fanstake closed a $6.25M seed led by Courtside Ventures with Susa Ventures, Will Ventures, Scrum Ventures, and Alumni Ventures, drawing two top sports-focused VC firms into one round. It targets the fast-growing NIL economy and partnered with The Collective Association.

name: Uplift Labs
founder: Sukemasa Kabayama, Jonathan Wills, Rahul Rajan
tagline: AI-powered markerless 3D motion capture that replaces expensive lab setups with just a smartphone for athlete performance and injury prevention.
why: Uplift Labs has raised over $8.5M from investors including SoftBank's Deepcore, Stadia Ventures, and athlete-angels such as NBA's Seth Curry and Marc Gasol. Led by ex-Tesla Japan president Masa Kabayama, it scaled from 12,000 to nearly 20,000 athletes in 2025 and serves MLB, NBA, and NCAA teams plus ESPN.

name: Hexis
tagline: An AI-driven athlete nutrition app that delivers personalized carb-coded fueling plans that adapt in real time to training load.
why: Hexis raised GBP 1.6M in pre-seed funding from athlete-backed VC firm APEX and global sports investor Sport Republic, with Olympic medallists and pro cyclists participating. Founded by sports-science PhDs David Dunne and Sam Impey, it applies peer-reviewed research to personalized performance nutrition.

name: ScorePlay
tagline: AI-powered media asset management platform that helps sports organizations centralize, tag, and distribute their content.
why: Used by 200+ leagues, federations and teams across the NBA, Premier League, LaLiga and NHL. Raised a $13M (EUR 12.5M) Series A in early 2025 with FIBA backing, and counts Alexis Ohanian as both a customer and investor. Co-founders were featured on Forbes 30 Under 30.

name: Jump
founder: Marc Lore, Alex Rodriguez, Jordy Leiser
tagline: Unified fan-experience and ticketing platform, described as the 'Shopify for sports teams and venues.'
why: Gives professional sports teams full control of ticketing, content, commerce and gameday operations in one platform. Raised $25M in Series A funding in October 2025, backed by Alexis Ohanian's Seven Seven Six, Courtside Ventures and Forerunner. Founding team includes high-profile operators Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez.

name: Surfe
tagline: Connected revenue workspace that syncs LinkedIn contacts and data directly into CRMs like Salesforce and HubSpot.
why: Bootstrapped to 1,500+ paying clients and EUR 1M ARR with no dedicated sales team before raising a EUR 4M seed. Used by revenue teams at Google and Uber, with strong product-led growth originating from a browser extension.

name: Vesence
tagline: AI agents that review law firms' documents, emails and projects in Microsoft Office before they are shared with clients.
why: Y Combinator-backed legal tech that raised a $9M seed in 2025. Targets the high-value, error-sensitive legal review workflow with AI agents embedded directly in Office. Featured in Sifted's B2B SaaS Rising 100.

name: Gradient Labs
founder: Dimitri Masin, Neal Lathia, Danai Antoniou
tagline: AI customer-support agent ('Otto') purpose-built for regulated industries such as financial services.
why: Founded by ex-Monzo data and ML leaders; scaled to GBP 1M ARR in three months. Doubled its Series A to $26M in 2025 as it expanded into the US. Its focus on safe, compliant AI for regulated operations is a clear differentiator.

name: Logic
tagline: AI-driven automation platform that lets business teams automate recurring review and decision processes without writing code.
why: Raised a $4.3M seed in October 2025, less than a year after founding. Targets no-code automation of repetitive decision workflows, a fast-growing category as foundation models lower the barrier to internal automation.

name: Wabi
tagline: A social platform, described as 'YouTube for apps,' where anyone can use prompts to instantly create and share mini-apps.
why: Founded by Replika creator Eugenia Kuyda, who has a track record building viral consumer AI. Raised an unusually large $20M pre-seed in November 2025, signaling strong investor conviction in consumer 'vibe-coding' platforms.

name: Aeon
tagline: AI-powered preventive health platform combining whole-body MRI, blood biomarker analysis and genetic testing for early disease detection.
why: Raised an EUR 8.2M seed in June 2025 led by Concentric. CEO Tim Seithe previously built and sold Tillhub to Unzer. Tackles preventable deaths through early detection, addressing a large and fast-growing preventive-health market.

name: Hormona
tagline: Femtech app and at-home testing that lets women track, understand and optimize their hormones.
why: Raised a GBP 5M seed in May 2025, co-led by Supernode Global and Voima Ventures. Award-winning app addressing an underserved women's health market, with at-home hormone testing and cycle forecasting. Earlier rounds were backed by Techstars and SFC Capital.

name: Peec AI
tagline: Analytics platform that helps brands track and improve how they appear in AI answer engines like ChatGPT.
why: As consumers shift from Google to ChatGPT, Peec AI helps brands monitor and optimize their visibility in LLM answers, a brand-new generative-engine-optimization category. Raised a $21M Series A led by Singular just four months after its seed round, one of the fastest follow-ons in the space.

name: Dazzle
tagline: Consumer tech startup from former Yahoo CEO Marissa Mayer focused on AI-powered consumer products.
why: Raised an $8M seed at a $35M valuation led by Forerunner's Kirsten Green, with Kleiner Perkins and Greycroft participating. Backed by top-tier consumer investors and led by a high-profile founder with deep consumer-product experience.

name: Series
tagline: AI-powered social network that runs entirely inside iMessage to make warm intro connections.
why: Founded by two Yale seniors who raised $5.1M pre-seed from Venmo co-founder Iqram Magdon-Ismail, Pear VC, Reddit CEO Steve Huffman, and GPTZero's Edward Tian. Native iMessage UX bypasses the app-install problem; total raised ~$8.2M while founders haven't dropped out.

name: Known
tagline: Voice-AI dating app that uses long onboarding conversations to broker real-life meetups.
why: Stanford-dropout founders raised $9.7M from Forerunner (its first dating investment), NFX, Pear VC and Coelius. In SF beta, 80% of intros led to in-person dates vs. low single digits on swipe apps — strong signal for AI-driven matchmaking.

name: Dub
tagline: Copy-trading investing app where Gen Z mirrors portfolios of top traders and politicians.
why: Harvard dropout/ex-Apple founder. $30M Series A co-led by Notable Capital and Neo, plus $5.5M SVB venture debt. Surpassed 1M downloads on viral 'follow Pelosi' feature; subscription model at $10/mo with sticky retention among teens.

name: Gensmo
tagline: AI fashion agent with real-time virtual try-on across 100M indexed products.
why: One of the largest consumer AI seed rounds in 2025 at $60M+. Founded by ex-Google Mobile Search tech lead and Alibaba VP with a Carnegie Mellon AI PhD. Beta drove 10M fashion matches and went viral on TikTok and Instagram pre-launch.

name: Phia
tagline: AI shopping agent that compares prices, summarizes products, and tracks resale value across the web.
why: Stanford roommates Phoebe Gates and Sophia Kianni raised $35M Series A from Notable Capital, Khosla, Kleiner Perkins. Hit 1M+ users, 6,200 brand partners, 11x revenue growth in under a year; backed by Hailey Bieber, Kris Jenner, Sara Blakely.

name: First Voyage
tagline: AI companion app Momo that helps users build healthy habits via a digital pet experience.
why: Backed by a16z speedrun, SignalFire, True Global. Momo users have created 2M+ tasks; popular for productivity, spirituality, mindfulness. Strong consumer AI safety story with prompt filters — well positioned in the $120M+ AI companion market.

name: Lemon Slice
tagline: Real-time interactive AI avatars generated from a single image for chatbots and creator tools.
why: $10.5M seed from Matrix Partners, Y Combinator, Dropbox CTO Arash Ferdowsi, Twitch CEO Emmett Shear and The Chainsmokers. Founding team is PhD-trained (MIT/Harvard/Stanford/Duke) with proprietary diffusion model — strong technical moat in AI video.

name: Sandbar
tagline: Stream, an AI voice-note smart ring for conversational notes and music control.
why: Founded by former Meta employees from CTRL-Labs (neural interface acquired by Meta). $23M Series A led by Adjacent and Kindred Ventures. Positioned squarely against Plaud/Omi as a beautiful, privacy-first wearable that isn't trying to be a fitness band.

name: Oboe
tagline: AI-powered course generation — users define a learning goal and AI creates the course.
why: Founded by Anchor co-founders and ex-Spotify execs Nir Zicherman and Michael Mignano. $16M Series A from a16z three months post-launch. Angels include Adam D'Angelo, Garry Tan, Lenny Rachitsky, and Mati Staniszewski — strong consumer-AI insider conviction.

name: Agentio
tagline: Programmatic creator-ad marketplace turning YouTubers into a buyable media channel.
why: Ex-Cameo president founder. $12M Series A led by Benchmark with Craft and AlleyCorp. 1,000+ creators, 50+ brands including DoorDash, Mint Mobile, MasterClass, Notion, HelloFresh. Forerunner-led $40M extension shows belief in creator-economy infrastructure.

name: Candle
tagline: Daily 5-minute connection app for couples and close friends — Duolingo for relationships.
why: YC-backed founders (ex-Apple, ex-Asana/Twitter) pivoted 5x before hitting 300K users + 150K couples in 6 months. DAU/MAU of ~50%, $150K MRR ($1M+ ARR). Backers: Goodwater, Pioneer Fund, Progression Fund, Y Combinator.

name: Joy
tagline: 24/7 parenting copilot via SMS combining real experts with AI insights.
why: $10M seed led by Forerunner; $14M Series A co-led by Forerunner and Raga Partners. Joy On Call SMS-first model at $8/month is a wedge into family infrastructure. Family/parenting is a Forerunner-anchored category.

name: Boop
tagline: AI travel app that turns social recommendations from friends and creators into bookable itineraries.
why: Founded by ex-Meta/Microsoft AR/VR lead Nancy Li Smith. Pre-seed co-led by Bling Capital and BBG Ventures, with Tripadvisor co-founder Stephen Kaufer and ex-Marriott president Stephanie Linnartz. Currently invite-only with active waitlist.

name: Airial
tagline: AI trip planner that turns travel TikToks into fully booked itineraries.
why: Founded by ex-Meta engineers (Karandikar worked at Meta/Google/Waymo; Shenoy built Instagram Reels). $3M seed led by Montage Ventures, South Park Commons, Peak XV. Uses DeepMind AlphaGeometry-inspired inference for complex multi-city travel logistics.

tagline: AI-curated IRL social experiences that pair strangers for dinners and activities.
why: USC research project turned full-stack social marketplace. $2.5M seed from 1517 Fund, General Catalyst, Best Nights VC, Upfront, Scrum Ventures, and Dropbox co-founder Arash Ferdowsi. Backed by trend of Gen Z fleeing swipe apps for IRL connection.

name: Adam
tagline: Text-to-3D AI copilot that turns prompts into editable CAD/3D objects.
why: One of YC W25's most viral startups, generating 10M+ social media impressions at launch. $4.1M seed from TQ Ventures, 468 Capital, Pioneer, Script Capital, and angels including Tim Glaser (PostHog), Trevor Blackwell (YC), and Theo Browne (T3 Chat).

name: Friend
tagline: Always-listening AI companion necklace pendant designed to combat loneliness.
why: Founder Avi Schiffmann (built the COVID tracker, Webby Award winner) raised ~$10M from Caffeinated Capital, Z Fellows, Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas, Solana founders, Morning Brew's Austin Rief. $129 device, 5K+ units sold, $1M NYC subway ad campaign generated mass cultural awareness.

name: Pinwheel
tagline: Kid-safe smartphones, smartwatches and AI chat with no social media and built-in parental controls.
why: 20x revenue growth since 2021 (Inc. 5000 top 5%). Top-rated kids phone in the US; expanding into UK, Canada, Australia. 2025 launched first kids smartwatch with parent-monitored AI chat. Owns the hardware + software stack for the $100B+ kids tech market.

name: Super Teacher
tagline: Consumer AI tutoring app for elementary-school students at $10–15/month subscription.
why: Founded by ex-Google PM and former Stuyvesant/Harlem math teacher Tim Novikoff (previously sold Fly Labs to Google). 20K+ paying families plus public schools in NY, NJ, HI. Startup Battlefield Top 20 at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025.

name: ReciMe
tagline: Recipe organization app letting home cooks save, share, and shop recipes from any source.
why: $1.5M seed led by Even Capital with Marissa Mayer as a notable backer. Crossed 500K home cooks in the US; relocated HQ to New York to scale into the largest US food-tech market. Riding the social-recipe + grocery-integration tailwind.

name: Outlit
tagline: AI agent for enterprise deal-making that turns deal history into a pricing and structuring playbook.
why: YC W25 alum building AI agents for Deal Desk and revenue ops. Founded by ex-enterprise SaaS operators selling to mid-market and enterprise sales teams. Featured in YC W25 batch coverage as a vertical AI standout.

name: Sennu AI
tagline: AI sales agent purpose-built for tech consulting firms, automating QA and Salesforce workflows.
why: YC W25 batch member. Vertical AI sales agent for IT consultancies — a fragmented, underserved B2B market. Featured on Product Hunt with early consulting-firm customers; backed by Y Combinator.

name: Ergo
tagline: Vertical AI agents for sales operations that automate CRM hygiene, deal review, and pipeline analytics.
why: YC W25 grad delivering specialized AI agents to revenue ops teams. Founders have engineering backgrounds at top tech companies; backed by Y Combinator and angels from the GTM software ecosystem.

name: Harper
tagline: AI-native commercial insurance brokerage that quotes and binds policies in 24–48 hours.
why: YC W25 grad raised $47M led by Emergence Capital — reportedly the largest publicly disclosed Series A by a Black founder. Serves 5,000+ businesses, processing 1,000+ deals/month vs. ~30/month for a traditional broker.

name: Trayd
tagline: Same-day payroll and back-office OS for specialty trade contractors.
why: Female-founded construction-tech raised a $10M Series A in three weeks led by White Star Capital, with YC doubling down. Reports 31x productivity gains for trade contractors; covered exclusively by Crunchbase News.

name: PermitFlow
tagline: TurboTax for construction permitting — AI handles research, submission, and tracking.
why: YC W22 alum; ARR grew 20x in 2023 and raised $31M Series A from Kleiner Perkins. Customers include national homebuilders and modular construction firms — vertical SaaS with a regulatory moat.

name: Unlimited Industries
founder: Alex Modon, Jordan Stern, Tara Viswanathan
tagline: AI construction company automating infrastructure design for data centers, energy, and advanced manufacturing.
why: $12M seed co-led by a16z and CIV in late 2025. Cuts pre-construction engineering from 6 months to a few weeks; cofounded by Rupa Health's Tara Viswanathan and Jordan Stern after Rupa's 2024 acquisition.

name: Juno
tagline: AI tax-prep platform that automates 90% of CPA busy work across 90+ document types.
why: CPA-founded; onboarded ~500 accounting firms in its first year and hit mid-seven-figure ARR within 8 months. $12M seed led by Bonfire Ventures (Crunchbase News exclusive, April 2026).

name: Risotto
founder: Aron Solberg, Alex Confer, Chris Paul
tagline: AI-native ITSM that autonomously resolves IT, HR, and RevOps tickets.
why: Customers include Gusto (60% of help queue automated), Jobber, Ironclad, and ThoughtSpot. $10M seed led by Bonfire Ventures (Jan 2026) with YC participation; covered by TechCrunch.

name: Kilo Code
tagline: Open-source AI coding agent platform that hit #1 on OpenRouter.
why: Co-founded by GitLab's Sid Sijbrandij and Brooklyn Data's Scott Breitenother. $8M seed announced December 2025; open-source coding agent reached #1 on OpenRouter with strong dev traction.

name: Keycard
founder: Ian Livingstone, Matthew Creager, Jared Hanson
tagline: Identity and access for AI agents — the auth layer for the agent economy.
why: $8M seed led by a16z and boldstart; $30M Series A led by Acrew. Founders previously scaled Snyk's dev security platform and Auth0/Okta — the playbook for identity-layer infrastructure.

name: Theo Ai
tagline: AI-powered litigation outcome and settlement prediction for Big Law.
why: $4.2M seed in May 2025 (NextView, Collide) following $2.2M pre-seed; raised additional $3.4M in Nov 2025. Selling into AmLaw 200 firms; Patrick Ip is a serial founder (Kit, ex-Google).

name: Soxton AI
tagline: AI-native legal platform automating early-stage legal services for startups.
why: Founded June 2025 by Harvard JD / ex-Cooley attorney Logan Brown. Emerged from stealth in Dec 2025 with $2.5M from Moxxie (Katie Stanton). Already served 500+ startups with 2,500 on the wait-list.

name: Inkeep
tagline: AI agent platform that turns product docs into trusted CX and ops teammates.
why: $13M seed led by Khosla in 2025 with YC and GreatPoint. MIT-founded; selling into developer-tools companies needing AI support agents grounded in their own docs and codebase.

name: Stack AI
tagline: No-code platform for enterprises to build and deploy AI agents.
why: MIT-PhD founded; raised $16M Series A in 2025 with angels including Vercel's Guillermo Rauch and Weaviate's Bob van Luijt. Customers include Fortune 500 enterprises deploying internal AI workflows.

name: WisdomAI
tagline: AI-native data analytics platform answering business questions across structured and unstructured data.
why: Founded by Rubrik cofounder Soham Mazumdar. $23M seed from Coatue, followed by $50M Series A led by Kleiner Perkins and Nvidia in Nov 2025, with a smart anti-hallucination approach.

name: Ridge AI
tagline: AI-native analytics letting B2B SaaS apps ship interactive customer dashboards and data agents in hours.
why: Founded by Tableau / Salesforce data-viz veteran Ellie Fields and Trifacta cofounder Jeff Heer. $2.6M pre-seed from Madrona (April 2026) with angels from Tableau, Streamlit, and Trifacta.

name: Definite
tagline: AI-native data stack collapsing warehouse, BI, and reverse-ETL into one platform.
why: Founded by Mike Ritchie, previously built SeekWell (acquired by ThoughtSpot). $10M seed led by Costanoa Ventures in August 2025; targets mid-market companies wanting analytics without months of data-engineering setup.

name: OpenObserve
tagline: Open-source AI-native observability — logs, metrics, and traces in one stack.
why: 6,000+ organizations on the open-source platform, including many Fortune 100s. $10M Series A led by Nexus Venture Partners and Dell Technologies Capital, announced April 2026.

name: Equal Parts
founder: Mike Witte, Mike Meller, Graham Yennie
tagline: InsurTech roll-up modernizing independent insurance agencies with AI.
why: Founded March 2025; raised $23M Series A led by Inspired Capital in Feb 2026. Reports ~40% revenue lift and ~50% bottom-line gain on acquired agencies; targeting 25 acquisitions in year one.

name: Pylon
founder: Marty Kausas, Advith Chelikani, Robert Eng
tagline: Customer support platform purpose-built for B2B teams across Slack, Teams, and email.
why: YC-backed, $17M Series A led by General Catalyst. Founding team comes from Airbnb, Samsara, and Affinity. Powers B2B support for fast-growing SaaS companies who outgrow Zendesk/Intercom.

name: Tofu
founder: EJ Cho, Elaine Zelby, Honglei Liu
tagline: Generative-AI omnichannel marketing platform for B2B enterprises.
why: $5M seed led by Index Ventures. Founders are ex-Slack, Twitter, Affirm, and Google ML. Building omnichannel campaign generation for enterprise B2B marketers replacing manual content + ABM tooling.

name: Subframe
tagline: AI design tool built for code — ship production-ready UI in minutes.
why: Pre-seed funded; founders previously at Robinhood and Coinbase. Code-native design tool seen as a fast-mover against Figma in AI-assisted UI generation; growing developer + design community.

name: QuiverAI
tagline: Frontier AI models for vector graphics (SVG) generation and editing.
why: $8.3M seed led by a16z (Yoko Li, Martin Casado) with angels including Webflow CEO Linda Tong and Replit's Amjad Masad. Team are authors of pioneering StarVector / RLRF research on SVG generation.

name: Persana AI
tagline: AI sales intelligence superapp combining 75+ data providers and intent signals.
why: YC-backed; LinkedIn / Shopify / Stripe alums. $2.3M seed with growing user base of B2B GTM teams aggregating 700M contacts and 200M companies for AI-driven prospecting and outbound automation.

name: Lotus Health AI
tagline: Free, insurance-free AI-driven primary care provider available 24/7 in 50+ languages.
why: Series A co-led by Kleiner Perkins and CRV with Joe Montana and former U.S. CTO Aneesh Chopra participating. Physician-led AI medical practice that makes physicians '10x more productive' by automating intake, record synthesis and administrative triage.

name: Amperos Health
founder: Michal Miernowski, Alvin Wu, Wilson Wang
tagline: AI-native denial management and revenue recovery platform for healthcare providers.
why: Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners with Uncork Capital and Neo. Serves 3,000+ clinical locations across all 50 states, driving nearly $700M in revenue recovered per year across 500,000+ claims.

name: Jimini Health
founder: Luis Voloch, Sahil Sud, Mark Jacobstein
tagline: AI-supported therapy platform pairing licensed clinicians with an AI assistant called Sage.
why: Pre-seed led by Zetta Venture Partners with PsyMed, LionBird, BoxGroup, and Arkitekt Ventures; recent $17M seed brings total to $25M. AI tool Sage prepares patients pre-session and integrates into the care plan under clinician oversight.

name: Ease Health
founder: Zach Cohen, Raymond Wang, Steve Gold
tagline: AI-native CRM, EHR and RCM operating system for behavioral health providers.
why: Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz. Consolidates 6-10 separate systems into one platform spanning referral to collections with AI automation across admissions, intake, documentation, utilization review and RCM. President Steve Gold previously built and sold Refresh to Optum.

name: Valerie Health
tagline: AI front office automating referrals, faxes and scheduling for independent provider groups.
why: Series A led by Redpoint with General Catalyst, Primary, BoxGroup, Karman, .406 Ventures. Revenue grew 5x in six months including 3x among existing customers. Co-founder Shalek previously co-founded Joyable (sold to AbleTo); Joshi co-founded Uber Health.

name: Translucent
tagline: First agentic AI platform for healthcare finance and CFO operations.
why: Series A led by GV with NEA, Virtue, and FPV Ventures, just months after a $7M seed. Consolidates operational, clinical and financial data into a unified view to monitor real-time signals and identify root causes. Serves health systems and medical groups across the US.

name: Blossom Health
tagline: AI copilot for psychiatry that augments clinicians and automates back-office work.
why: Round led by Headline with Village Global, TA Ventures, Operator Partners. Tools used by hundreds of clinicians treating 10,000+ patients across multiple states; most patients seen within 48 hours. Founder previously at Athelas and EverQuote.

name: Cofertility
tagline: Tech-enabled fertility ecosystem offering zero-cost egg freezing through donation matching.
why: Series A co-led by Next Ventures and Offline Ventures with Initialized Capital, Foreground Capital, How Women Invest, Gaingels. Reimagines egg freezing economics via a donation-matching model. Co-founder Tecco previously founded Rock Health.

name: Generation Lab
founder: Alina Su, Irina Conboy, Michael Suswal
tagline: SystemAge longevity diagnostic measuring biological age across multiple organ systems.
why: Seed led by Accel (its first longevity bet) with Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki, Giannis Antetokounmpo and Simu Liu. Top 1% of all startup seed raises. CSO Conboy is a tenured Berkeley professor and parabiosis pioneer.

name: Circulate Health
tagline: Therapeutic plasma exchange longevity network to clear inflammation and aging biomarkers.
why: Seed led by Khosla Ventures with Seaside and CSC Ventures. Already partnered with 24 clinics across 8 states, targeting 50+. Clinical trial showed TPE+IVIG reduced biological age by avg 2.6 years. Co-founder Verdin is CEO of the Buck Institute for Research on Aging.

name: HEARTio
founder: Utkars Jain, Adam Butchy, Michael Leasure
tagline: ECGio AI platform that detects coronary artery disease from a standard 12-lead ECG.
why: Seed backed by Intelligence Ventures, Audacious Capital, VU Venture Partners, LifeX, Bessel, Shepherd. Cross-validation study of 16,000+ patients published in JACC Advances. Founders earned Forbes 30 Under 30 recognition. Capital funds pivotal study for FDA clearance.

name: Keebler Health
founder: Isaac Park, Andrew Stickney, Kevin Hill, Terrell Bacchus
tagline: AI platform unlocking unstructured clinical data for risk adjustment and value-based care.
why: Series A led by Flare Capital with Sands Capital and Tau Ventures; total funding now $22M. Builds AI-powered infrastructure powering risk adjustment, population health and RADV audit readiness for value-based care organizations nationwide.

name: Yuzu Health
founder: Max Kauderer, Ryan Lee, Russell Pekala
tagline: Modern infrastructure layer for health plans and benefits administrators.
why: Series A led by General Catalyst and Chemistry. Helps health plans and insurance brokers launch and scale benefit designs by modernizing antiquated insurer plumbing. Backed by General Catalyst's broader healthcare thesis around insurance infrastructure.

name: Develop Health
tagline: GenAI-powered prior authorization and medication access platform integrated into EHRs.
why: Series A led by Wing Venture Capital with Afore, J Ventures, South Park Commons. First medication-access platform architected around GenAI with 12+ purpose-built LLM pipelines. Founders previously built provider-facing tools at Canvas Medical and Rupa Health.

name: Coral
tagline: AI agents that read handwritten fax forms, process prior auths and complete patient intakes in under five minutes.
why: Series A led by Lightspeed and Z47. Document models operate at 99.7% accuracy on handwritten faxes, scanned insurance cards and PA templates. Multiple millions in ARR within a year of commercial launch; targeting 4x growth in 2026.

name: Anterior
tagline: AI that helps health insurers review and process prior authorization requests faster.
why: Series A led by NEA at a $95M post-money valuation with Sequoia and Neo participating. Founder is a former physician who left medicine to build AI for prior auth review. Selected for Original Medicare AI Prior Authorization pilot.

name: HeyDonto AI
tagline: Dental interoperability exchange connecting dental practice management with medical EHRs.
why: Seed closed at a $200M valuation led by eight private investors who are practicing dentists and dental industry veterans. Conduit platform uses patented semantic intelligence to harmonize data across dental and medical systems.

name: Enzo Health
tagline: AI-driven platform automating home health front office, clinical ops and back office.
why: Series A led by N47 with Gradient, Tandem Ventures and Rigby Watts. Posted a 40x revenue jump over the past year. Used by organizations collectively supporting 500,000+ patients annually. Expanding into adjacent skilled nursing and hospice.

name: XCaliber Health
tagline: Agentic operating system orchestrating provider workflows across every system a clinic touches.
why: Seed led by ManchesterStory with Benhamou Global Ventures and Arka Venture Labs. Already processes 8M+ chart updates and generates 160,000+ EHR updates daily across 700,000+ unique patients. Patient navigator agent saves providers ~6 hours/day on prescription refills alone.

name: Counsel Health
tagline: Physician-supervised AI front door delivering messaging-based virtual care in minutes.
why: Series A co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and GV with Asymmetric, Floodgate and Pear VC; follows an $11M seed from a16z. Named to 2026 New York Digital Health 100. Founder is Stanford MD and former CMO of Notable Health.

name: Health Universe
tagline: HIPAA-compliant AI agent infrastructure for academic medical centers and health systems.
why: Seed led by Kleiner Perkins. At Duke Clinical Research Institute, Project Loom accelerated clinical trial setup 30-40x (from 6-9 months to ~7.5 days). ONC B.11 certified, TEFCA participant, SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA-aligned.

name: Layer Health
founder: David Sontag, Steven Horng, Monica Agrawal
tagline: LLM-powered platform that automates complex clinical chart review and abstraction.
why: Series A led by Define Ventures with GV, Flare Capital and MultiCare Capital. Spun out of MIT with founders from MIT, Harvard, Microsoft and Google. AI outperforms humans on complex chart abstraction across all major registries. Customers include MultiCare.

name: Evvy
founder: Priyanka Jain, Laine Bruzek, Pita Navarro
tagline: At-home vaginal microbiome testing leveraging metagenomic sequencing and precision care.
why: Series A led by Left Lane Capital with General Catalyst, Labcorp Venture Fund, RH Capital, BBG Ventures. First and only at-home vaginal microbiome test using metagenomic sequencing. Launched a 3-in-1 probiotic in 2025. Backed by Labcorp as a strategic partner.

name: Stipple Bio
tagline: Precision oncology drug discovery via the Pointillist Platform identifying tumor-specific cell surface epitopes.
why: Series A co-led by RA Capital, a16z Bio+Health and Nextech Invest with Emerson Collective (Yosemite), GV, LoLa, GordonMD. Lead candidate STP-100 advancing to Phase 1 in 2027. Co-founders are professors at Fred Hutch and UCSF.

name: Sully.ai
tagline: Autonomous team of AI agents (Receptionist, Scribe, Triage Nurse, Coder) running entire hospital operations.
why: Series A from AUM Ventures, Leonis Capital, SemperVirens, and Sequoia Scout Fund; Y Combinator S21. AutonomousOS automates clinical documentation, admin workflows and RCM. Independent benchmarks show Sully outperforming tech giants in healthcare AI accuracy.

name: Splash Sports
tagline: Peer-to-peer, real-money fantasy sports contest platform that powers commissioner-led group games.
why: Series A investors include Acies Investments, Elysian Park Ventures, Accomplice, plus angels Jonathan Kraft (Patriots), Theo Epstein, and Alex Morgan. Platform launched in 33 states with major distribution partners.

name: Otto Sport AI
tagline: AI-powered operating system for youth sports teams covering club management, leagues, ticketing, and athlete recruitment.
why: Seed co-led by Mamba Growth Equity and Rally Ventures including three strategic acquisitions at launch. Founding team includes veterans from SportsEngine, TeamSnap, NCSA, and FloSports. Targets the $30-40B annual US youth sports market with AI-first workflow tools.

name: Outlier
tagline: Subscription sports betting analytics platform delivering real-time stats, historical trends, and odds comparison.
why: Series A led by Discerning Capital with $5M equity + $5M user acquisition financing. Tens of thousands of paying subscribers across US, UK and Australia with 14,000+ five-star App Store reviews. Subscription-only model with no affiliate revenue.

name: Greenfly
tagline: Short-form digital media platform giving teams, leagues, athletes and talent real-time access to game content for fan distribution.
why: Customers include MLB, ESPN, NHL, Dodgers, San Jose Sharks, and Paris Saint-Germain. NBA All-Star Chris Paul is an investor; co-founded by former MLB All-Star Shawn Green. Acquired Miro AI to expand curation capabilities.

name: Rec
tagline: Modern operating system for local parks and community recreation departments enabling residents to discover, book, and pay for activities.
why: Series A led by Crosslink Capital with athlete investors Larry Fitzgerald Jr., Kelvin Beachum Jr., Kevin Durant/Rich Kleiman's 35V, Joe Montana's Liquid 2 Ventures, and Marquee Ventures (Chicago Cubs ownership). Targets the $1.9T US recreation industry running on outdated software.

name: SwingVision
tagline: AI-powered single-camera platform delivering automated stats, highlights and line-calling for tennis and pickleball.
why: Series A led by Authentic Ventures with pro tennis stars Lindsay Davenport, Alison Riske, and Rohan Bopanna plus Glenn Solomon of GGV. Founders are ex-Tesla Autopilot and ex-LinkedIn/Cisco. Targets 23.6M US tennis players and expansion into pickleball.

name: Gemini Sports Analytics
tagline: No-code AI and ML platform enabling pro teams to build predictive models for roster management and tactical optimization.
why: Backed by Roger Ehrenberg, Raptor Group, and Will Ventures. Active multi-year, six-figure contracts with NFL's Indianapolis Colts and NCAA's Texas A&M Aggies. Founder Jake Schuster is a former strength and conditioning coach across nine sports in seven countries.

name: StatusPro
tagline: VR sports technology platform whose breakout title NFL Pro Era brings first-person football simulation to Meta Quest.
why: Series A led by GV (Google Ventures) with Dream Sports, Wise Ventures, Alumni Ventures, LeBron James, Drake, Maverick Carter, Myles Garrett, and Haslam Sports. NFL Pro Era surpassed 1M users as fastest-selling VR sports title on Meta's Quest.

name: Nextiles
tagline: Materials science company embedding force-sensitive sensor threads into fabric to quantify athletic performance.
why: Seed led by Drive by DraftKings with the NBA, Madison Square Garden Sports Corp., Alumni Ventures, SmartSports, Phoenix Capital Ventures, Newlab, and Olympic ice hockey medalist Hilary Knight. Patented fabric-based sewing technology with NSF and Air Force SBIR grants.

name: PredictionStrike
tagline: Sports stock market platform where users buy and sell athlete shares in a real-money market that fluctuates like an exchange.
why: Series A led by Bullpen Capital with MaC Venture Capital, Sixty8 VC, Correlation Ventures, and Gaingels. Platform has processed $60M+ in transactions and crossed 175,000 users. Differentiated B2C product in the fantasy/betting infrastructure adjacency.

name: Youth Inc
founder: Greg Olsen, Ryan Baise, Tim Murphy
tagline: First digital media network dedicated exclusively to youth sports content and direct-to-consumer commerce.
why: Seed led by Will Ventures with Ryan Sports Ventures, Hello Sunshine co-founder Jim Toth, and ISOS Ventures. Co-founded by Fox Sports broadcaster and former NFL TE Greg Olsen, ex-Fanatics exec Ryan Baise, and ex-Audacy/NYT exec Tim Murphy. Podcast features Jerry Rice, Russell Wilson, Jennie Finch.

name: Ankored
tagline: Safety and compliance platform helping youth sports organizations manage credentials, certifications and abuse-prevention requirements.
why: Seed led by Rally Ventures with Justin Kaufenberg (SportsEngine co-founder/former CEO) joining the board. Addresses a 2,780% increase in SafeSport misconduct reports since 2017. Integrates with leading youth sports management platforms.

name: Pro Athlete Community
tagline: Education, mentorship, and peer-network platform helping current and former pro athletes pursue purpose-driven post-career lives.
why: Series A led by GSV Ventures with Maveron. Membership doubled to 650+ current/former pro athletes across 10+ leagues including ~10% of all currently active NFL players. Co-founder Chip Paucek is the edtech veteran who built 2U.

name: College Sports Company
founder: Mike Grieve, Adam Breneman, Andrew Spano
tagline: Athlete-driven media production company that builds custom media networks for top athletic departments and NIL collectives.
why: Seed led by Crosslink Capital with Boston Seed, Tom Brady's Shadow Lion, NBA All-Star Kevin Love, former Bleacher Report CEO Brian Grey, Bleacher Report co-founder Dave Nemetz, and Morning Brew co-founder Austin Reif. Networks live at Penn State, Tennessee, BYU, Louisville and South Carolina.

name: Mustard
tagline: AI-powered motion analysis and coaching app for baseball pitching and golf swing improvement.
why: Seed investors include Mark Cuban, NFL legend Ronnie Lott, and pro golfer Justin Rose. Computer-vision technology personalized by pitching guru Dr. Tom House. Launched Mustard Golf in partnership with Golf Digest as the first AI-powered platform for understanding and improving golf swings.

name: NOCAP Sports
founder: Nicholas Lord, Casey Floyd, Stan Golubtsov
tagline: Athlete influencer marketplace and NIL platform connecting brands with college athletes across all NCAA divisions.
why: Seed led by Florida Funders with Florida Institute, College Hunks Hauling Junk CEO Omar Soliman, and an executive from Wasserman. Partnered with university programs and recently launched with Exos a Transfer Portal Combine in Dallas.

name: Out2Win
tagline: AI-powered athlete marketing intelligence platform helping brands, agencies and universities vet and partner with college athletes.
why: Investors include Barbara Jones of Outshine Talent, Daniel Lovitz of MLS, and Michael Gibbons of Fox Sports. Database of 250,000+ collegiate athletes powering the proprietary Out2Win Score. Customers include Rhoback, Olipop, Accelerator Active Energy, Michigan State and San Diego State.

name: AthleteAgent
tagline: IMDb-style athlete database and marketplace connecting marketers with athletes and agents for endorsement deals.
why: Backed by Aaron Rodgers (seven-figure check), sports agent Scott Boras, Solyco Capital, Prota Ventures, Bullock Capital, and Pressplay Capital. Database holds 4,000+ athletes and 2,000+ agents across NBA, NHL, NFL, MLB and MLS. Subscription model at $30/month with six-figure revenue pace.

name: SportsVisio
tagline: Mobile-first generative AI platform automating stats, analytics, and video highlights for basketball from a single phone camera.
why: Seed led by Hyperplane Venture Capital. Team includes veteran ex-DARPA personnel with sports backgrounds. SV Hoops product used by universities, leagues, schools, and national tech companies. New Hampshire-based with dozens of engineers focused on computer-vision for sports.

name: Fantasy Life
tagline: Fantasy sports and gaming media platform with original content, tools, and the Guillotine Leagues format.
why: Seed led by LeBron James/Maverick Carter's LRMR Ventures and SC Holdings. Revenue up 737% since 2022 launch with 230% jump from 2024 to first half of 2025. Founder Matthew Berry is the long-time NBC Sports/ESPN fantasy football analyst.

name: PlayMaker
tagline: AI-powered partnership management platform automating sports sponsorship operations, contracts, and partner workflows.
why: Pre-seed led by Precursor Ventures with Antler and Incisive Ventures. Closed 5 customers in first six weeks post-launch with $250k+ in pipeline. Founder Hannah Sorkin spent six years at Anheuser-Busch leading partnerships with major sports teams and leagues.

name: Raidium
tagline: The 'GPT of radiology' — an AI-native foundation model that reads CT scans and MRIs in seconds, the way a radiologist would.
why: Founded by a radiologist and a data scientist from Owkin, Raidium built the world's first 3D large vision-language model for medical imaging. Their €16M seed co-led by Newfund and Kurma (Nov 2024) — with EIC backing — puts them at the frontier of applied clinical AI. Radiology is one of the highest-value, most AI-ready specialties in healthcare.

name: Lempire
tagline: A bootstrapped suite of B2B growth tools — lemlist, Taplio, Tweet Hunter — built to help businesses scale through outreach and content.
why: Guillaume Moubeche scaled Lempire to $45M ARR with a 90-person team and $10M EBITDA — zero primary VC. The portfolio model (multiple SaaS tools under one brand) is rare and defensible, and the build-in-public playbook has created an outsized distribution engine that most funded competitors can't replicate.

name: GrowSurf
tagline: Plug-and-play referral program software that turns your existing customers into your best acquisition channel.
why: Kevin Yun grew GrowSurf to $831K ARR with six employees and zero VC funding — profitable from day one in a market dominated by well-funded competitors. A textbook example of disciplined, product-led bootstrapping: the company publishes revenue publicly and compounds through word-of-mouth of the very product it sells.

name: Robeauté
tagline: A grain-of-rice-sized microrobot that navigates inside the brain, giving surgeons precision access to treat tumors and neurological conditions.
why: One of the most technically audacious European medtech bets in years. Backed by Plural, Cherry Ventures, and Kindred Ventures, with Brainlab (a leading surgical device maker) as strategic co-investor — dramatically de-risking the clinical and commercialization path. CEO Bertrand Duplat previously founded and sold Virtools to Dassault Systèmes.

name: Pro Padel League
tagline: The first professional padel league in North America, with 10 teams across the US, Canada, and Mexico.
why: Padel is the world's fastest-growing racket sport, with 30M+ players globally and explosive US momentum. PPL has first-mover advantage on the professional league structure, a Charlotte Hornets co-chairman-led Series A, and Gary Vaynerchuk and Left Lane Capital on cap table. The 2026 season opens in NYC this July.

name: Somnee
founder: Dr. Matthew Walker, Dr. Robert Knight, Tim Rosa
tagline: AI-powered sleep headband that reads your brainwaves and delivers personalized electrical stimulation to guide you into deep sleep faster.
why: Co-founded by the world's most cited sleep scientist — Dr. Matthew Walker, author of Why We Sleep — alongside UC Berkeley neuroscientists and ex-Fitbit exec Tim Rosa. NBA teams are already using it for athlete recovery. Khosla-led with Marc Benioff's TIME Ventures and the Adidas family fund. Rare convergence of scientific credibility and consumer distribution.

name: Mimo
tagline: All-in-one financial management platform for SMBs: get paid, pay suppliers, and see your real-time cash flow — all in one place.
why: Henrik Grim scaled Capchase across Europe as GM, then had Northzone (his former employer as an investor) lead Mimo's seed round — a signal of rare conviction. Mimo attacks the fragmented SMB payments stack with a simple payments-in / payments-out model that maps to how small businesses actually think about money.

name: Boardy
tagline: An AI that calls you on the phone, learns who you are, and makes warm introductions to the exact right people — automatically.
why: Boardy famously closed its own $8M seed round autonomously — the AI conducted investor calls without Andrew in the room. That's the ultimate product dogfood story. Creandum-led, with a genuinely novel phone-first UX for professional networking that no other product has attempted at scale.

name: Dust
tagline: AI agent platform that connects to your company's tools (Slack, Notion, GitHub) and deploys purpose-built assistants for every team function.
why: Stanislas Polu spent years on OpenAI's research team before co-founding Dust with former Alan Head of Product Gabriel Hubert. Sequoia backed them from seed through Series A — rare multi-round conviction from the world's top AI-focused VC. 1,000+ enterprise customers including Alan, Qonto, and Doctolib with €2M ARR growing triple digits.

name: Output Sports
founder: Dr. Martin O'Reilly, Dr. Darragh Whelan
tagline: A single wearable sensor that gives strength coaches and sports scientists lab-grade athlete performance data — anywhere, anytime.
why: Output Sports tech drove medal-winning performances at the 2024 Paris Olympics, including Ireland's first-ever swimming gold. Captured 30 million athlete measurements globally in 2024. A UCD spinout backed by Atlantic Bridge and Enterprise Ireland, now expanding into the US after proving elite adoption at the highest level of global sport.

name: Lago
tagline: Open-source usage-based billing infrastructure for SaaS — the developer-friendly alternative to Stripe Billing, built for AI-era pricing models.
why: Two early Qonto employees (Anh-Tho was employee #1) building the billing layer every usage-based SaaS eventually needs to own. YC S21, backed by FirstMark and SignalFire. As AI products shift to consumption pricing, Lago's open-source moat and ~$100M valuation at Series A suggests massive headroom ahead.

name: Cal.com
tagline: Open-source scheduling infrastructure that lets any company embed calendar booking natively — without sending users to a third-party tool.
why: Cal.com hit 50,000+ GitHub stars and became the go-to open-source Calendly alternative in under two years. Backed by 776 (Alexis Ohanian) and Obvious Ventures, with a community-driven GTM that dramatically lowers CAC. Peer Richelsen's previous company was acquired by WeWork's Flatiron School before he turned his attention to the scheduling problem.

name: Graza
tagline: Single-origin Spanish olive oil in a squeezy bottle — designed to be cooked with, not saved for a special occasion.
why: Hit $100K in revenue their first week, $19.7M by year two, $48M+ by year three — all on roughly $1M in seed capital. The genius was the product itself: a squeeze bottle that makes premium olive oil approachable and daily. Now in Whole Foods and Target. One of the most capital-efficient DTC brand-building stories in recent memory.

name: Fishwife
tagline: Beautifully tinned, sustainably sourced fish — turning a forgotten pantry staple into something you're proud to put on the table.
why: Almost single-handedly revived the $2.6B US tinned fish category through obsessive branding and sustainability storytelling. Bootstrapped and angel-backed, now in Whole Foods nationally. Becca Millstein made Inc.'s Female Founders 250 list and appeared on Shark Tank. A masterclass in repositioning a commodity category through brand.

name: Vacation Inc.
founder: Marty Bell, Lach Hall, Dakota Green
tagline: Award-winning sunscreen that smells like a vacation — on a mission to make SPF the most desirable product in your beach bag.
why: Built an $80M retail sales brand on pure creative marketing and nostalgia — no complex tech, just the right product, aesthetic, and brand story. $6M Series A led by Silas Capital, VMG Partners later came in at a $210M valuation. Turned a commoditized SPF category into a lifestyle object. Proof that taste and brand conviction can build a defensible consumer business.

name: Polar
tagline: Merchant-of-record payment infrastructure for AI-native developers — handle billing, taxes, and subscriptions globally from day one.
why: Founded by ex-Shopify Director of Product (and Tictail founder, acquired by Shopify in 2018), Polar's $10M seed was led by Accel with angels including Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke, Vercel's Guillermo Rauch, and Supabase's Paul Copplestone. 16,000 developers on the platform with average sales growing 120%+ month-on-month. Perfectly timed for the wave of solo AI developers who need Stripe-grade infrastructure without the complexity.

name: Resend
tagline: The modern email API for developers — beautiful transactional emails that actually land in the inbox, not spam.
why: 200,000+ developers signed up in 18 months, powering millions of emails per day for brands like Warner Brothers and Decathlon. $18M Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz (Dec 2024) validates the thesis that developer email infrastructure is broken and ripe for a clean-sheet rebuild. YC W23 pedigree and obsessive developer experience design make Resend the Stripe of email.

name: Cavela
tagline: The world's first AI product sourcing agent — automating supplier matching, negotiation, and order delivery for consumer brands.
why: Anthony Sardain grew up across Southeast Asian trade hubs in a family with three generations in materials trade, then built an AI agent that reduces 1,000 sourcing touch points to 10. Seed co-led by XYZ Venture Capital and Susa Ventures (Nov 2025). 200,000+ vetted suppliers across 40+ countries, cutting production costs by an average of 35%. With tariff uncertainty reshaping global supply chains, Cavela's timing is surgical.

name: Liveblocks
tagline: Real-time collaboration infrastructure that lets any product team add Figma-like multiplayer features — cursors, comments, notifications — in hours, not months.
why: Boldstart-led with Seedcamp and angels from Figma, GitHub, Vercel, and Segment backing the bet. Liveblocks is the picks-and-shovels play for the collaboration economy — every SaaS product eventually needs multiplayer, and building it from scratch is brutally hard. Two French founders quietly building the infrastructure layer that makes every app feel alive.

name: Ghia
tagline: A beautifully designed non-alcoholic aperitif that gives you the ritual of a cocktail without the hangover — the Aesop of the sober-curious movement.
why: Ex-Glossier Head of Retail Melanie Masarin raised her first $900K from Emily Weiss and her network in a single week, then scaled Ghia to $7M+ revenue and a $50M valuation. Appeared on Shark Tank Season 14. Retail sales up 132% in 2024. Ghia rides the massive sober-curious wave ($1.3T non-alcoholic beverage market) with brand design and taste that make not drinking feel aspirational, not restrictive.

name: MUD\WTR
tagline: Mushroom-based coffee alternative with cacao, chai, and adaptogens — designed to give you energy and focus without the jitters or crash.
why: Shane Heath put $25K of his home down-payment money into building MUD\WTR, raised just $1M in seed from friends and family, then scaled to $60M+ projected revenue by year three and 130,000+ monthly subscribers. No Series A, no growth equity, no dilution — just a single product that tapped into the anti-caffeine-dependency zeitgeist. One of the most capital-efficient consumer brand stories of the decade.

name: Sycai Medical
founder: Sara Toledano, Javier García, Júlia Rodríguez-Comas
tagline: AI-powered medical device that detects early-stage cancer and pre-malignant lesions in the abdomen from standard imaging — no invasive test required.
why: Top-funded European Medical Diagnostics seed in 2025, led by LUMO Labs and Ship2B Ventures, plus a €5M Horizon Europe grant. CE-marked under MDR for pancreatic cancer detection (summer 2024) with a successful first clinical trial in 2023. Pancreatic cancer has a 12% five-year survival rate largely because it's caught too late — Sycai's non-invasive AI screening could fundamentally change early detection economics.
